vulnerability.title: Ensure PAM Enforces Password Requirements - Minimum Uppercase Characters
vulnerability.description: The pam_pwquality module's ucredit= parameter controls requirements for usage of uppercase letters in a password. When set to a negative number, any password will be required to contain that many uppercase characters. When set to a positive number, pam_pwquality will grant +1 additional length credit for each uppercase character. Modify the ucredit setting in /etc/security/pwquality.conf to require the use of an uppercase character in passwords.
vulnerability.recommendation: Remediation script: # Remediation is applicable only in certain platforms if rpm --quiet -q kernel-core && { rpm --quiet -q libpwquality; }; then var_password_pam_ucredit='' if grep -sq ucredit /etc/security/pwquality.conf.d/*.conf ; then sed -i "/ucredit/d" /etc/security/pwquality.conf.d/*.conf fi # Strip any search characters in the key arg so that the key can be replaced without # adding any search characters to the config file. stripped_key=$(sed 's/[\^=\$,;+]*//g' <<< "^ucredit") # shellcheck disable=SC2059 printf -v formatted_output "%s = %s" "$stripped_key" "$var_password_pam_ucredit" # If the key exists, change it. Otherwise, add it to the config_file. # We search for the key string followed by a word boundary (matched by \>), # so if we search for 'setting', 'setting2' won't match. if LC_ALL=C grep -
vulnerability.group: Set Password Quality Requirements with pam_pwquality

triage_decision.reason: This is a standard STIG password policy hardening change for PAM. It involves a simple configuration edit to /etc/security/pwquality.conf to require uppercase characters in passwords. The fix is unlikely to cause system breakage or lockout as it only affects new password changes, not existing passwords. Follows recommended STIG patterns for authn/authz hardening.
triage_decision.estimated_impact: authentication/authorization affected; Potential user lockouts if existing passwords are not updated to meet new requirements; Requires validation after change to ensure passwords comply

reasoning_messages.reasoning:
The user wants me to remediate an OpenSCAP finding related to PAM password requirements. The finding is about ensuring PAM enforces password requirements for minimum uppercase characters. Specifically, the rule is accounts_password_pam_ucredit (Rule ID: xccdf_org.ssgproject.content_rule_accounts_password_pam_ucredit).

According to the remediation context:
- This involves editing /etc/security/pwquality.conf to set the ucredit parameter
- The ucredit parameter controls requirements for uppercase letters in passwords
- When set to a negative number, it requires that many uppercase characters
- When set to a positive number, it grants length credits for uppercase characters
- The recommendation is to require the use of an uppercase character in passwords

I need to:
1. First read the current /etc/security/pwquality.conf file to see the current ucredit setting
2. Then modify it appropriately (likely set to -1 to require at least one uppercase character)
3. Use run_cmd, read_file, write_file tools appropriately

Let me start by reading the file to see the current state.
